content:
# Exodus Chapter 8 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~35r–36r (PDF pp. 68–69); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 48–49 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_008_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_008_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_008_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_008_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

## Plague of Frogs (vv. 1–15)

**1** And YHWH said to Mosheh: "Go in to Par'oh, and you shall say to him, 'Thus says YHWH: Send out my people, and let them serve me.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה בֹּא אֶל־פַּרְעֹה וְאָמַרְתָּ אֵלָיו כֹּה אָמַר יְהוָה שַׁלַּח אֶת־עַמִּי וְיַעַבְדֻנִי (bo el-Par'oh, "go in to Par'oh"; shalakh et-ammi ve-ya'avduni, "send out my people and let them serve me"). **Versification note:** This is MT 7:26. English versification begins chapter 8 here. LXX (Vaticanus): εἴσελθε πρὸς Φαραω καὶ ἐρεῖς πρὸς αὐτόν· τάδε λέγει κύριος· ἐξαπόστειλον τὸν λαόν μου ἵνα μοι λατρεύσωσιν ("go in to Pharao and say to him: thus says the Lord: send out my people that they may serve me").

**2** And if you [are] refusing to send out, behold, I [am] striking all your territory with frogs [tsefarde'im].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאִם־מָאֵן אַתָּה לְשַׁלֵּחַ הִנֵּה אָנֹכִי נֹגֵף אֶת־כָּל־גְּבוּלְךָ בַּצְפַרְדְּעִים (im-ma'en attah le-shale'akh, "if you [are] refusing to send out"; nogef et-kol-gevulkha ba-tsefarde'im, "striking all your territory with frogs"). The word צְפַרְדְּעִים (tsefarde'im) = "frogs," etymology debated — possibly Egyptian or onomatopoeic. LXX: ἐγὼ τύπτω πάντα τὰ ὅριά σου βατράχοις ("I strike all your borders with frogs") — βάτραχος = "frog."

**3** And the Ye'or [Nile] shall swarm [with] frogs, and they shall go up and come into your house, and into the room of your lying-down, and upon your bed, and into the house of your servants, and upon your people, and into your ovens, and into your kneading-troughs.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשָׁרַץ הַיְאֹר צְפַרְדְּעִים וְעָלוּ וּבָאוּ בְּבֵיתֶךָ וּבַחֲדַר מִשְׁכָּבְךָ וְעַל־מִטָּתֶךָ וּבְבֵית עֲבָדֶיךָ וּבְעַמְּךָ וּבְתַנּוּרֶיךָ וּבְמִשְׁאֲרוֹתֶיךָ (ve-sharats ha-Ye'or tsefarde'im, "and the Nile shall swarm [with] frogs"; ba-khadar mishkavkha, "into the room of your lying-down"; be-tannureicha, "into your ovens"; u-ve-mish'aroteicha, "and into your kneading-troughs"). LXX: καὶ ἐξερεύξεται ὁ ποταμὸς βατράχους ("and the river shall pour forth frogs") — ἐξερεύγομαι ("vomit/pour forth") is more vivid than the Hebrew שָׁרַץ ("swarm").

**4** And upon you, and upon your people, and upon all your servants, the frogs shall go up.'"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּבְכָה וּבְעַמְּךָ וּבְכָל־עֲבָדֶיךָ יַעֲלוּ הַצְפַרְדְּעִים (u-vekha u-ve-ammekha u-ve-khol-avadeicha ya'alu ha-tsefarde'im, "and upon you and upon your people and upon all your servants the frogs shall go up"). LXX: καὶ ἐπὶ σὲ καὶ ἐπὶ τοὺς θεράποντάς σου καὶ ἐπὶ τὸν λαόν σου ἀναβήσονται οἱ βάτραχοι ("and upon you and upon your servants and upon your people the frogs shall go up").

**5** And YHWH said to Mosheh: "Say to Aharon, 'Stretch out your hand with your rod over the rivers, over the canals, and over the pools, and bring up the frogs upon the land of Mitsrayim.'"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה אֱמֹר אֶל־אַהֲרֹן נְטֵה אֶת־יָדְךָ בְּמַטֶּךָ עַל־הַנְּהָרֹת עַל־הַיְאֹרִים וְעַל־הָאֲגַמִּים וְהַעַל אֶת־הַצְפַרְדְּעִים עַל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (neteh et-yadkha be-mattekha, "stretch out your hand with your rod"; al-ha-neharot al-ha-ye'orim ve-al-ha-agammim, "over the rivers, over the canals/Nile-branches, and over the pools"; ve-ha'al, hiphil imperative of 'alah, "bring up"). LXX: ἔκτεινον τὴν χεῖρά σου ἐν τῇ ῥάβδῳ σου ἐπὶ τοὺς ποταμοὺς καὶ ἐπὶ τὰς διώρυγας καὶ ἐπὶ τὰ ἕλη ("stretch out your hand with your rod over the rivers and over the canals and over the marshes").

**6** And Aharon stretched out his hand over the waters of Mitsrayim, and the frog [ha-tsefardea'] came up and covered the land of Mitsrayim.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֵּט אַהֲרֹן אֶת־יָדוֹ עַל מֵימֵי מִצְרָיִם וַתַּעַל הַצְּפַרְדֵּעַ וַתְּכַס אֶת־אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (vata'al ha-tsefardea', "and the frog came up"; va-tekhas, "and it/she covered"). **Grammatical note:** The Hebrew uses the singular הַצְּפַרְדֵּעַ (ha-tsefardea', "the frog") — a collective singular, treating the entire plague as a single mass. LXX: καὶ ἀνεβιβάσθη ὁ βάτραχος ("and the frog was brought up") — also uses the singular βάτραχος, preserving the Hebrew collective.

**7** And the chartummim [magicians] did so with their latim [secret arts], and they brought up the frogs upon the land of Mitsrayim.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעֲשׂוּ־כֵן הַחַרְטֻמִּים בְּלָטֵיהֶם וַיַּעֲלוּ אֶת־הַצְפַרְדְּעִים עַל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (be-lateihem, "with their secret arts" — again the shorter form לָטִים/lat rather than לַהֲטִים/lahatim from 7:11; vaya'alu, "and they brought up"). The chartummim replicate the plague but cannot remove it — they only add to it. LXX: ἐποίησαν δὲ ὡσαύτως καὶ οἱ ἐπαοιδοὶ τῶν Αἰγυπτίων ταῖς φαρμακείαις αὐτῶν ("and the enchanters of Egypt did likewise with their sorceries").

**8** And Par'oh called to Mosheh and to Aharon and said: "Pray to YHWH, and let him turn aside the frogs from me and from my people, and I will send out the people, and let them sacrifice to YHWH."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּקְרָא פַרְעֹה לְמֹשֶׁה וּלְאַהֲרֹן וַיֹּאמֶר הַעְתִּירוּ אֶל־יְהוָה וְיָסֵר הַצְפַרְדְּעִים מִמֶּנִּי וּמֵעַמִּי וַאֲשַׁלְּחָה אֶת־הָעָם וְיִזְבְּחוּ לַיהוָה (ha'tiru, hiphil imperative of 'atar, "pray/entreat"; ve-yaser, "and let him turn aside/remove"; va-ashalkhah, "and I will send out"; ve-yizbekhu, "and let them sacrifice"). This is the first time Par'oh asks for intercession — his request uses sacrificial language (זבח) rather than עבד ("serve"). LXX: εὔξασθε περὶ ἐμοῦ πρὸς κύριον ("pray concerning me to the Lord") — εὔχομαι for 'atar.

**9** And Mosheh said to Par'oh: "Glorify yourself over me: for when shall I pray for you, and for your servants, and for your people, to cut off the frogs from you and from your houses; only in the Ye'or [Nile] shall they remain?"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר מֹשֶׁה לְפַרְעֹה הִתְפָּאֵר עָלַי לְמָתַי אַעְתִּיר לְךָ וְלַעֲבָדֶיךָ וּלְעַמְּךָ לְהַכְרִית הַצְפַרְדְּעִים מִמְּךָ וּמִבָּתֶּיךָ רַק בַּיְאֹר תִּשָּׁאַרְנָה (hitpa'er alai, "glorify yourself over me" — i.e., "have the honor over me [of choosing the time]"; le-matai, "for when?"; le-hakhrit, hiphil infinitive of karat, "to cut off"; raq ba-Ye'or tisha'arnah, "only in the Nile shall they remain"). LXX: τάξαι πρός με πότε εὔξωμαι περὶ σοῦ ("appoint for me when I shall pray for you") — renders hitpa'er as τάξαι ("appoint/set").

**10** And he said: "Tomorrow." And he said: "According to your word — so that you may know that there is none like YHWH our God.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר לְמָחָר וַיֹּאמֶר כִּדְבָרְךָ לְמַעַן תֵּדַע כִּי־אֵין כַּיהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ (le-makhar, "tomorrow"; ki-dvarekha, "according to your word"; le-ma'an teda', "so that you may know"; ki-ein ka-YHWH eloheinu, "that there is none like YHWH our God"). Par'oh chooses "tomorrow" rather than "now" — often interpreted as residual stubbornness or testing. LXX: εἰς αὔριον... κατὰ τὸ ῥῆμά σου· ἵνα εἰδῇς ὅτι οὐκ ἔστιν ἄλλος πλὴν κυρίου ("tomorrow... according to your word, that you may know that there is no other except the Lord").

**11** And the frogs shall turn aside from you, and from your houses, and from your servants, and from your people; only in the Ye'or [Nile] shall they remain."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְסָרוּ הַצְפַרְדְּעִים מִמְּךָ וּמִבָּתֶּיךָ וּמֵעֲבָדֶיךָ וּמֵעַמֶּךָ רַק בַּיְאֹר תִּשָּׁאַרְנָה (ve-saru, "and they shall turn aside/depart"; raq ba-Ye'or tisha'arnah, "only in the Nile shall they remain"). LXX: καὶ περιαιρεθήσονται οἱ βάτραχοι ἀπὸ σοῦ ("and the frogs shall be removed from you").

**12** And Mosheh and Aharon went out from Par'oh; and Mosheh cried out to YHWH concerning the matter of the frogs which he had set upon Par'oh.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֵּצֵא מֹשֶׁה וְאַהֲרֹן מֵעִם פַּרְעֹה וַיִּצְעַק מֹשֶׁה אֶל־יְהוָה עַל־דְּבַר הַצְפַרְדְּעִים אֲשֶׁר־שָׂם לְפַרְעֹה (vayits'aq, "and he cried out"; al-devar ha-tsefarde'im, "concerning the matter of the frogs"; asher-sam le-Far'oh, "which he had set/placed upon Par'oh"). LXX: καὶ ἐβόησεν Μωυσῆς πρὸς κύριον περὶ τοῦ ὁρισμοῦ τῶν βατράχων ("and Moses cried to the Lord concerning the appointment/limit of the frogs") — ὁρισμός ("appointment/decree") interprets devar differently.

**13** And YHWH did according to the word of Mosheh, and the frogs died from the houses, from the courtyards, and from the fields.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעַשׂ יְהוָה כִּדְבַר מֹשֶׁה וַיָּמֻתוּ הַצְפַרְדְּעִים מִן־הַבָּתִּים מִן־הַחֲצֵרֹת וּמִן־הַשָּׂדֹת (vayamutu ha-tsefarde'im, "and the frogs died"; min-ha-battim min-ha-khatserot u-min-ha-sadot, "from the houses, from the courtyards, and from the fields"). LXX: καὶ ἐτελεύτησαν οἱ βάτραχοι ἐκ τῶν οἰκιῶν καὶ ἐκ τῶν ἐπαύλεων καὶ ἐκ τῶν ἀγρῶν ("and the frogs died from the houses and from the farmsteads and from the fields").

**14** And they gathered them [in] heaps, heaps; and the land stank.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּצְבְּרוּ אֹתָם חֳמָרִם חֳמָרִם וַתִּבְאַשׁ הָאָרֶץ (vayitsberu otam khomarim khomarim, "and they gathered them heaps, heaps"; va-tiv'ash ha-arets, "and the land stank"). The repetition חֳמָרִם חֳמָרִם is emphatic — "heaps upon heaps." LXX: καὶ συνήγαγον αὐτοὺς θιμωνιὰς θιμωνιάς, καὶ ὤζεσεν ἡ γῆ ("and they gathered them heaps upon heaps, and the land stank").

**15** And Par'oh saw that there was relief, and he made heavy [kaved] his heart, and he did not listen to them, as YHWH had spoken.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּרְא פַּרְעֹה כִּי הָיְתָה הָרְוָחָה וְהַכְבֵּד אֶת־לִבּוֹ וְלֹא שָׁמַע אֲלֵהֶם כַּאֲשֶׁר דִּבֶּר יְהוָה (ki haytah ha-revakhah, "that there was the relief/respite"; ve-hakhbed et-libbo, "and he made heavy his heart"). **Hardening verb: kaved (כבד) = "make heavy."** The form הַכְבֵּד (hakhbed) is the hiphil infinitive absolute functioning as a finite verb — Par'oh is the agent who makes his own heart heavy. This is the first time in the narrative sequence where Par'oh actively hardens his own heart using kaved in the hiphil. LXX: καὶ ἐβάρυνεν Φαραω τὴν καρδίαν αὐτοῦ ("and Pharao made heavy his heart") — βαρύνω corresponds to kaved hiphil.

---

## Plague of Gnats/Lice (vv. 16–19)

**16** And YHWH said to Mosheh: "Say to Aharon, 'Stretch out your rod and strike the dust of the land, and it shall become kinnim [gnats/lice] in all the land of Mitsrayim.'"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה אֱמֹר אֶל־אַהֲרֹן נְטֵה אֶת־מַטְּךָ וְהַךְ אֶת־עֲפַר הָאָרֶץ וְהָיָה לְכִנִּם בְּכָל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (neteh et-mattekha, "stretch out your rod"; ve-hakh et-afar ha-arets, "and strike the dust of the land"; ve-hayah le-khinnim, "and it shall become kinnim"). The word כִּנִּם (kinnim) is debated: "gnats," "lice," or "mosquitoes." The root may relate to כֵּן (ken, "base/pedestal"), suggesting a swarming base-creature. LXX: σκνῖφες (skniphes, "gnats/mosquitoes") — a stinging insect, not lice.

**17** And they did so; and Aharon stretched out his hand with his rod and struck the dust of the land, and the kinnim [gnats/lice] were on the adam [man/humanity] and on the beast; all the dust of the land became kinnim in all the land of Mitsrayim.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעֲשׂוּ־כֵן וַיֵּט אַהֲרֹן אֶת־יָדוֹ בְמַטֵּהוּ וַיַּךְ אֶת־עֲפַר הָאָרֶץ וַתְּהִי הַכִּנָּם בָּאָדָם וּבַבְּהֵמָה כָּל־עֲפַר הָאָרֶץ הָיָה כִנִּם בְּכָל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (va-tehi ha-kinnam ba-adam u-va-behemah, "and the gnats/lice were on the man and on the beast"; kol-afar ha-arets hayah khinnim, "all the dust of the land became gnats/lice"). LXX: καὶ ἐγένοντο οἱ σκνῖφες ἐν τοῖς ἀνθρώποις καὶ ἐν τοῖς τετράποσιν ("and the gnats were on the men and on the four-footed beasts") — renders behemah as τετράπους ("four-footed").

**18** And the chartummim [magicians] did so with their latim [secret arts] to bring forth the kinnim [gnats/lice], and they were not able; and the kinnim were on the adam [man/humanity] and on the beast.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעֲשׂוּ־כֵן הַחַרְטֻמִּים בְּלָטֵיהֶם לְהוֹצִיא אֶת־הַכִּנִּים וְלֹא יָכֹלוּ וַתְּהִי הַכִּנָּם בָּאָדָם וּבַבְּהֵמָה (le-hotsi et-ha-kinnim, "to bring forth the gnats/lice"; ve-lo yakholu, "and they were not able"). **Critical narrative turning point:** The chartummim FAIL for the first time. They could replicate blood (7:22) and frogs (8:7) but cannot produce kinnim. This is the limit of their latim. LXX: ἐποίησαν δὲ ὡσαύτως καὶ οἱ ἐπαοιδοὶ ταῖς φαρμακείαις αὐτῶν ἐξαγαγεῖν τὸν σκνῖφα, καὶ οὐκ ἠδύναντο ("and the enchanters did likewise with their sorceries to bring forth the gnat, and they were not able").

**19** And the chartummim [magicians] said to Par'oh: "A finger of Elohim [God] [is] this." And the heart of Par'oh was strengthened [chazaq], and he did not listen to them, as YHWH had spoken.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמְרוּ הַחַרְטֻמִּם אֶל־פַּרְעֹה אֶצְבַּע אֱלֹהִים הִוא וַיֶּחֱזַק לֵב־פַּרְעֹה וְלֹא־שָׁמַע אֲלֵהֶם כַּאֲשֶׁר דִּבֶּר יְהוָה (etsba' Elohim hi, "a finger of God [is] this"; vayekhezaq lev-Par'oh, "and the heart of Par'oh was strengthened"). **Hardening verb: chazaq (חזק) = "strengthen."** Qal — Par'oh's heart strengthened itself, even after his own magicians confess divine action. The chartummim say אֱלֹהִים (Elohim, "God") — not YHWH. They acknowledge a divine agent but use the generic term. LXX: δάκτυλος θεοῦ ἐστιν τοῦτο ("a finger of God is this") — θεός for Elohim. καὶ ἐσκληρύνθη ἡ καρδία Φαραω ("and the heart of Pharao was hardened") — again uses σκληρύνω for chazaq.

---

## Plague of Flies/Swarms (vv. 20–32)

**20** And YHWH said to Mosheh: "Rise early in the morning and stand before Par'oh — behold, he goes out to the water — and you shall say to him, 'Thus says YHWH: Send out my people, and let them serve me.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה הַשְׁכֵּם בַּבֹּקֶר וְהִתְיַצֵּב לִפְנֵי פַרְעֹה הִנֵּה יוֹצֵא הַמָּיְמָה וְאָמַרְתָּ אֵלָיו כֹּה אָמַר יְהוָה שַׁלַּח אֶת־עַמִּי וְיַעַבְדֻנִי (hashkem ba-boqer, "rise early in the morning"; ve-hityatsev, "and station yourself/stand"; hineh yotse ha-maymah, "behold, he goes out to the water"). This echoes 7:15 — Par'oh goes to the water, perhaps a ritual or daily washing at the Ye'or. LXX: ὄρθρισον τὸ πρωῒ καὶ στῆθι ἐναντίον Φαραω ("rise early in the morning and stand before Pharao").

**21** For if you do not send out my people, behold, I [am] sending upon you, and upon your servants, and upon your people, and into your houses, the 'arov [swarm]; and the houses of Mitsrayim shall be full of the 'arov, and also the ground upon which they [are] upon it.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כִּי אִם־אֵינְךָ מְשַׁלֵּחַ אֶת־עַמִּי הִנְנִי מַשְׁלִיחַ בְּךָ וּבַעֲבָדֶיךָ וּבְעַמְּךָ וּבְבָתֶּיךָ אֶת־הֶעָרֹב וּמָלְאוּ בָּתֵּי מִצְרַיִם אֶת־הֶעָרֹב וְגַם הָאֲדָמָה אֲשֶׁר־הֵם עָלֶיהָ (mashlikh bekha... et-he-'arov, "sending upon you... the swarm"; u-male'u battei Mitsrayim et-he-'arov, "and the houses of Mitsrayim shall be full of the swarm"). The word עָרֹב ('arov) is traditionally "swarm of flies" but its root means "mixture/mixed swarm." It may denote a diverse swarm of biting insects or creatures. LXX: τὴν κυνόμυιαν ("the dog-fly") — a specific biting fly (Stomoxys or similar), more specific than the Hebrew.

**22** And I will set apart on that day the land of Goshen upon which my people [are] standing upon it, that the 'arov [swarm] shall not be there, so that you may know that I [am] YHWH in the midst of the land.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהִפְלֵיתִי בַיּוֹם הַהוּא אֶת־אֶרֶץ גֹּשֶׁן אֲשֶׁר עַמִּי עֹמֵד עָלֶיהָ לְבִלְתִּי הֱיוֹת־שָׁם עָרֹב לְמַעַן תֵּדַע כִּי אֲנִי יְהוָה בְּקֶרֶב הָאָרֶץ (ve-hifleti, hiphil of palah, "and I will set apart/distinguish"; et-erets Goshen, "the land of Goshen"; le-vilti heyot-sham 'arov, "that the swarm shall not be there"; be-qerev ha-arets, "in the midst of the land"). **First explicit mention of distinction between Yisra'el and Mitsrayim in the plagues.** The root פלה (palah) = "to set apart, distinguish" — a key theological verb. LXX: καὶ παραδοξάσω ἐν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ ἐκείνῃ τὴν γῆν Γεσεμ ("and I will make wondrous on that day the land of Gesem") — παραδοξάζω captures the wonder of the distinction.

**23** And I will set a pedut [ransom/division] between my people and your people; tomorrow this sign shall be.'"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשַׂמְתִּי פְדֻת בֵּין עַמִּי וּבֵין עַמֶּךָ לְמָחָר יִהְיֶה הָאֹת הַזֶּה (ve-samti pedut bein ammi u-vein ammekha, "and I will set a ransom/distinction between my people and your people"). The word פְּדֻת (pedut) = "ransom/redemption" (from פדה, padah, "to ransom") — some manuscripts and ancient versions read פְּלֻת (pelut, "division/distinction"). The Masoretic text has pedut, suggesting the distinction itself is an act of redemption. LXX: καὶ δώσω διαστολὴν ἀνὰ μέσον τοῦ ἐμοῦ λαοῦ καὶ ἀνὰ μέσον τοῦ σοῦ λαοῦ ("and I will set a distinction between my people and your people") — διαστολή ("distinction/division") supports the reading "division."

**24** And YHWH did so; and a heavy [kaved] 'arov [swarm] came into the house of Par'oh and [into] the house of his servants, and in all the land of Mitsrayim the land was ruined from the face of the 'arov.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעַשׂ יְהוָה כֵּן וַיָּבֹא עָרֹב כָּבֵד בֵּיתָה פַרְעֹה וּבֵית עֲבָדָיו וּבְכָל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם תִּשָּׁחֵת הָאָרֶץ מִפְּנֵי הֶעָרֹב ('arov kaved, "a heavy swarm" — note the use of כָּבֵד (kaved) as adjective for the swarm, same root as the hardening verb; tissakhet ha-arets, "the land was ruined/corrupted"; mippenei he-'arov, "from the face of the swarm"). LXX: καὶ παρεγένετο ἡ κυνόμυια πλῆθος εἰς τοὺς οἴκους Φαραω ("and the dog-fly came in abundance into the houses of Pharao") — πλῆθος ("abundance") for kaved. καὶ ἐξωλεθρεύθη ἡ γῆ ἀπὸ τῆς κυνομυίης ("and the land was destroyed from the dog-fly").

**25** And Par'oh called to Mosheh and to Aharon and said: "Go, sacrifice to your God in the land."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּקְרָא פַרְעֹה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה וּלְאַהֲרֹן וַיֹּאמֶר לְכוּ זִבְחוּ לֵאלֹהֵיכֶם בָּאָרֶץ (lekhu zivkhu le-loheikhem ba-arets, "go, sacrifice to your God in the land"). Par'oh's first concession — but he restricts the sacrifice to within the land (ba-arets), not the wilderness as YHWH demands. LXX: δεῦτε θύσατε τῷ θεῷ ὑμῶν ἐν τῇ γῇ ("come, sacrifice to your God in the land").

**26** And Mosheh said: "It is not right to do so, for the to'avah [abomination] of Mitsrayim we sacrifice to YHWH our God; behold, [if] we sacrifice the to'avah of Mitsrayim before their eyes, will they not stone us?

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר מֹשֶׁה לֹא נָכוֹן לַעֲשׂוֹת כֵּן כִּי תּוֹעֲבַת מִצְרַיִם נִזְבַּח לַיהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ הֵן נִזְבַּח אֶת־תּוֹעֲבַת מִצְרַיִם לְעֵינֵיהֶם וְלֹא יִסְקְלֻנוּ (lo nakhon la'asot ken, "it is not right to do so"; to'avat Mitsrayim, "the abomination of Mitsrayim"; nizbach, "we sacrifice" — the thing sacrificed IS what Mitsrayim considers abominable; yisqelunu, "will they [not] stone us"). The to'avah likely refers to cattle/sheep that were sacred or taboo in Egyptian religion. LXX: τὰ γὰρ βδελύγματα τῶν Αἰγυπτίων θύσομεν κυρίῳ τῷ θεῷ ἡμῶν ("for the abominations of the Egyptians we shall sacrifice to the Lord our God") — βδέλυγμα = "abomination."

**27** A way of three days we will go into the wilderness, and we will sacrifice to YHWH our God as he says to us."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads דֶּרֶךְ שְׁלֹשֶׁת יָמִים נֵלֵךְ בַּמִּדְבָּר וְזָבַחְנוּ לַיהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ כַּאֲשֶׁר יֹאמַר אֵלֵינוּ (derekh sheloshet yamim, "a way of three days"; nelekh ba-midbar, "we will go into the wilderness"; ka-asher yomar eleinu, "as he says to us"). LXX: ὁδὸν τριῶν ἡμερῶν πορευσόμεθα εἰς τὴν ἔρημον ("a way of three days we shall journey into the wilderness").

**28** And Par'oh said: "I will send you out, and you shall sacrifice to YHWH your God in the wilderness; only going far you shall not go far. Pray for me."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר פַּרְעֹה אָנֹכִי אֲשַׁלַּח אֶתְכֶם וּזְבַחְתֶּם לַיהוָה אֱלֹהֵיכֶם בַּמִּדְבָּר רַק הַרְחֵק לֹא־תַרְחִיקוּ לָלֶכֶת הַעְתִּירוּ בַּעֲדִי (anokhi ashalakh etkhem, "I will send you out"; raq harkhek lo-tarkhiqu lalekhet, "only going far you shall not go far to go" — emphatic infinitive absolute + finite verb; ha'tiru ba'adi, "pray for me/on my behalf"). Par'oh's second concession — allows wilderness worship but limits the distance. LXX: ἐγὼ ἀποστέλλω ὑμᾶς... ἀλλὰ μὴ μακρὰν ἀποτείνητε πορευθῆναι ("I send you... but do not stretch far to go"). εὔξασθε οὖν περὶ ἐμοῦ πρὸς κύριον ("pray therefore for me to the Lord").

**29** And Mosheh said: "Behold, I [am] going out from you, and I will pray to YHWH, and the 'arov [swarm] shall turn aside from Par'oh, from his servants, and from his people tomorrow; only let not Par'oh again deal deceptively, by not sending out the people to sacrifice to YHWH."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר מֹשֶׁה הִנֵּה אָנֹכִי יוֹצֵא מֵעִמָּךְ וְהַעְתַּרְתִּי אֶל־יְהוָה וְסָר הֶעָרֹב מִפַּרְעֹה מֵעֲבָדָיו וּמֵעַמּוֹ מָחָר רַק אַל־יֹסֵף פַּרְעֹה הָתֵל לְבִלְתִּי שַׁלַּח אֶת־הָעָם לִזְבֹּחַ לַיהוָה (ve-ha'tarti, "and I will pray/entreat"; ve-sar, "and it shall turn aside"; al-yosef Par'oh hatel, "let not Par'oh again deal deceptively" — hatel from תלל, "to deceive/mock"). Mosheh warns Par'oh against the pattern of promising release during the plague and then reneging when relief comes. LXX: μὴ προσθῇς ἔτι Φαραω ἐξαπατῆσαι ("let not Pharao again deceive") — ἐξαπατάω ("deceive utterly").

**30** And Mosheh went out from Par'oh and prayed to YHWH.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֵּצֵא מֹשֶׁה מֵעִם פַּרְעֹה וַיֶּעְתַּר אֶל־יְהוָה (vaye'tar el-YHWH, "and he prayed/entreated to YHWH"). LXX: καὶ ηὔξατο πρὸς τὸν θεόν ("and he prayed to God") — note θεόν ("God") rather than κύριον ("Lord"), a minor variant.

**31** And YHWH did according to the word of Mosheh, and he turned aside the 'arov [swarm] from Par'oh, from his servants, and from his people; not one remained.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעַשׂ יְהוָה כִּדְבַר מֹשֶׁה וַיָּסַר הֶעָרֹב מִפַּרְעֹה מֵעֲבָדָיו וּמֵעַמּוֹ לֹא נִשְׁאַר אֶחָד (vayasar he-'arov, "and he turned aside the swarm"; lo nish'ar ekhad, "not one remained"). LXX: καὶ περιεῖλεν κύριος τὴν κυνόμυιαν ἀπὸ Φαραω... οὐ κατελείφθη οὐδεμία ("and the Lord removed the dog-fly from Pharao... not one was left").

**32** And Par'oh made heavy [kaved] his heart also at this time, and he did not send out the people.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּכְבֵּד פַּרְעֹה אֶת־לִבּוֹ גַּם בַּפַּעַם הַזֹּאת וְלֹא שִׁלַּח אֶת־הָעָם (vayakhbed Par'oh et-libbo, "and Par'oh made heavy his heart"; gam ba-pa'am ha-zo't, "also at this time"; ve-lo shilakh et-ha-am, "and he did not send out the people"). **Hardening verb: kaved (כבד) = "make heavy."** Hiphil — Par'oh is again the agent who makes his own heart heavy/dull. This mirrors v.15 exactly. LXX: καὶ ἐβάρυνεν Φαραω τὴν καρδίαν αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐπὶ τοῦ καιροῦ τούτου ("and Pharao made heavy his heart also at this time") — βαρύνω for kaved hiphil, consistent with v.15.

---

## Hardening-Verb Tracking — Exodus 8

| Verse | Hebrew Root | Verb Form | Translation | Agent |
|-------|-----------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| 8:15 | כבד (kaved) | Hiphil inf. abs. הַכְבֵּד | "he made heavy" | Par'oh (self) |
| 8:19 | חזק (chazaq) | Qal וַיֶּחֱזַק | "was strengthened" | Par'oh's heart (self) |
| 8:32 | כבד (kaved) | Hiphil וַיַּכְבֵּד | "he made heavy" | Par'oh (self) |

### Cumulative Hardening-Verb Summary (Exodus 7–8)

| Verse | Hebrew Root | Verb Form | Translation | Agent |
|-------|-----------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| 7:3 | קשה (qashah) | Hiphil impf. אַקְשֶׁה | "I will make hard" | YHWH (predictive) |
| 7:13 | חזק (chazaq) | Qal וַיֶּחֱזַק | "was strengthened" | Par'oh's heart (self) |
| 7:14 | כבד (kaved) | Adjective כָּבֵד | "[is] heavy" | Par'oh's heart (state) |
| 7:22 | חזק (chazaq) | Qal וַיֶּחֱזַק | "was strengthened" | Par'oh's heart (self) |
| 8:15 | כבד (kaved) | Hiphil inf. abs. הַכְבֵּד | "he made heavy" | Par'oh (self) |
| 8:19 | חזק (chazaq) | Qal וַיֶּחֱזַק | "was strengthened" | Par'oh's heart (self) |
| 8:32 | כבד (kaved) | Hiphil וַיַּכְבֵּד | "he made heavy" | Par'oh (self) |

**Pattern note:** Through Exodus 7–8, YHWH has NOT YET actively hardened Par'oh's heart in the narrative sequence. The prediction in 7:3 uses qashah (YHWH as future agent), but every actual hardening so far is either Par'oh's heart strengthening itself (chazaq qal) or Par'oh actively making his own heart heavy (kaved hiphil). The shift to YHWH as active agent comes later in the plague narrative.

## Key Lexical Notes — Exodus 8

- **tsefarde'im / tsefardea'** (צְפַרְדְּעִים / צְפַרְדֵּעַ, vv. 1–14): "frogs" — v.6 uses the collective singular הַצְּפַרְדֵּעַ ("the frog"), treating the entire mass as one entity. LXX: βάτραχος / βάτραχοι.
- **kinnim** (כִּנִּם / כִּנָּם, vv. 16–19): "gnats" or "lice" — identity disputed. LXX σκνῖφες ("gnats/mosquitoes"). This is the first plague the chartummim cannot replicate.
- **'arov** (עָרֹב, vv. 20–32): "swarm" — traditionally rendered "flies" but the root ערב means "to mix," suggesting a "mixed swarm." LXX renders as κυνόμυια ("dog-fly"), a specific biting insect.
- **chartummim** (חַרְטֻמִּים, vv. 7, 18, 19): The magicians continue to use their latim (לָטִים, "secret arts") — they succeed in 8:7 (frogs) but fail in 8:18 (kinnim), prompting their confession of "the finger of God" (8:19).
- **pedut** (פְּדֻת, v. 23): "ransom/redemption/distinction" — from פדה ("to ransom"). The theological weight of using a redemption term for the act of setting apart Yisra'el from Mitsrayim is significant.
- **to'avah** (תּוֹעֲבָה, v. 26): "abomination" — what Yisra'el sacrifices (cattle/sheep) is what Mitsrayim considers abominable; sacrificing in Mitsrayim would provoke violence.
- **Ye'or** (יְאֹר): The Nile — continues as a key setting; the frog plague originates from the Ye'or (vv. 3, 5, 9, 11).
